You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: c0066b4a).

## Issue to Resolve

Remove default for allowed wildcard imports in `ktlint_official` codestyle
>Expected Behavior
> When java.util.* is included as a wildcard import, it should trigger the warning for no-wildcard-imports.
> ...
> Given that, I think it makes sense that ktlint should stop including those two paths as default wildcard exceptions.

_Originally posted by @greg-patterson in https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1792#issuecomment-1407098815_

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

The feature to respect setting ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand  was implemented not too long ago in ktlint 0.45.x via https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1272. The reason for adding the default values is that they seems to be hard-coded in IntelliJ IDEA as well (https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82).

If the property is not set explicitly, IntelliJ IDEA allows imports java.util.* and kotlinx.android.synthetic.*. If ktlint would not do the same, this would result in a conclict between ktlint and IntelliJ IDEA. Lots of users of ktlint dislike that.

In the new codestyle `ktlint_official` the default value for allowed wildcard imports shoulbe be cleared.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.editorconfig`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `docs/rules/standard.md`
- `ktlint-cli/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/cli/internal/KtlintCommandLine.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/IndentationRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoWildcardImportsRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.importordering.PatternEntry
  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97→            )
 98→    }
 99→}
100→
101→public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId

CHANGELOG.md:41:The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
--
CHANGELOG.md:43:The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.

CHANGELOG.md:229:* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.

 39→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {

 41→    }
 42→
 43→    fun executeRule(
 44→        rule: Rule,
 45→        fqRuleId: String,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        try {
 50→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 51→            rule.beforeFirstNode(

 53→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 54→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 55→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties),
 56→            )

 59→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 60→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 61→                e.line,
 62→                e.col,
 63→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 64→                """

 66→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 67→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 68→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 69→                """.trimIndent(),

104→                            }

106→                }
107→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
108→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                }
110→            } catch (e: Exception) {
111→                if (autoCorrect) {
112→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the

114→                    throw RuleExecutionException(

123→                    throw RuleExecutionException(

125→                        line,
126→                        col,
127→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
128→                        e.cause ?: e,
129→                    )
130→                }
131→            }
132→        }
133→    }

135→    companion object {
136→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
137→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
138→            code: Code,
139→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
140→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine.isInvokedFromCli)
141→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
142→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
143→
144→            val psiFileName =
145→                code.fileName
146→                    ?: if (code.script) {
147→                        "File.kts"
148→                    } else {
149→                        "File.kt"
150→                    }
151→            val psiFile = psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
152→                psiFileName,
153→                KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
154→                normalizedText,
155→            ) as KtFile
156→
157→            psiFile
158→                .findErrorElement()
159→                ?.let { errorElement ->
160→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
161→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
162→                }
163→
164→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
165→
166→            val editorConfig =
167→                ktLintRuleEngine
168→                    .editorConfigLoader
169→                    .load(code.filePath)
170→                    .also {
171→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
172→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
173→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
174→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
175→                    }
176→
177→            val ruleRunners =
178→                ktLintRuleEngine.ruleRunners(
179→                    RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),

182→

 52→     * Properties specified in [editorConfigOverride] take precedence above any other '.editorconfig' file on [filePath]
 53→     * or default value.

 55→    internal fun load(filePath: Path?): EditorConfig {
 56→        val editorConfigPath = filePath ?: defaultFilePath()
 57→        val editorConfigProperties: MutableMap<String, Property> =
 58→            createResourcePropertiesService(editorConfigLoaderEc4j.editorConfigLoader, editorConfigDefaults)
 59→                .queryProperties(editorConfigPath.resource())
 60→                .properties
 61→        return editorConfigProperties
 62→            .also { properties ->
 63→                if (properties[TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME]?.sourceValue == properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.name]?.sourceValue &&
 64→                    editorConfigOverride.properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY] != null
 65→                ) {
 66→                    // The tab_width property can not be overridden via the editorConfigOverride. So if it has been
 67→                    // set to the same value as the indent_size property then keep its value in sync with that
 68→                    // property.
 69→                    properties[TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME] =
 70→                        Property
 71→                            .builder()
 72→                            .name(TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME)
 73→                            .type(PropertyType.tab_width)
 74→                            .value(editorConfigOverride.properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY]?.source)
 75→                            .build()
 76→                }
 77→                editorConfigOverride
 78→                    .properties
 79→                    .forEach {
 80→                        properties[it.key.name] = it.key.toPropertyWithValue(it.value)
 81→                    }

 83→                LOGGER.debug { properties.prettyPrint(filePath) }

 99→                         */
100→                        EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY,
101→                    )
102→            }
103→    }

105→    private fun MutableMap<String, Property>.prettyPrint(normalizedFilePath: Path?) =
106→        map { entry -> "${entry.key}: ${entry.value.sourceValue}" }
107→            .joinToString(
108→                prefix = "Effective editorconfig properties${
109→                    if (normalizedFilePath == null) {
110→                        ""
111→                    } else {
112→                        " for file '$normalizedFilePath'"
113→                    }
114→                }:\n\t",
115→                separator = "\n\t",
116→            )
117→
118→    private fun Path?.resource() = Resource.Resources.ofPath(this, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
119→
120→    private fun defaultFilePath() =
121→        fileSystem
122→            .getPath(".")
123→            .toAbsolutePath()
124→            .resolve(SUPPORTED_FILES.first())

126→    private fun createResourcePropertiesService(
127→        editorConfigLoader: EditorConfigLoader,
128→        editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults,

151→/**
152→ * Wrapper around the native EditorConfigLoader of the ec4j library. Whenever Ktlints loads ".editorconfig" files via the ec4j library, it
153→ * needs to be aware of all native (ec4j) but also the custom property types defined in KtLint and the custom rules sets provided to KtLint.
154→ *
155→ * The ec4j library uses the convention that a property type has a name that is identical to the name of that property. If no
156→ * property type is registered for a property the property will be returned with a null type. When retrieving the property value it will be
157→ * returned as type "String" which results in class cast exceptions in KtLint's EditorConfig whenever the expected type of the variable is
158→ * not of type String.
159→ */
160→internal class EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(
161→    private val propertyTypes: Set<PropertyType<*>>,
162→) {
163→    val editorConfigLoader: EditorConfigLoader
164→        get() = EditorConfigLoader.of(Version.CURRENT, propertyTypeRegistry(propertyTypes))
165→
166→    private fun propertyTypeRegistry(propertyTypes: Set<PropertyType<*>>) =
167→        PropertyTypeRegistry
168→            .builder()
169→            .defaults()
170→            .apply {
171→                propertyTypes.forEach { type(it) }
172→            }.build()
173→}

CHANGELOG.md:41:The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.

14→ * - Kotlin specific properties defined
15→ *   [here](https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/master/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java)
16→ */

18→    /**
19→     * Type of property. Could be one of default ones (see [PropertyType.STANDARD_TYPES]) or custom one.
20→     */
21→    public val type: PropertyType<T>,
22→
23→    /**

25→     * unless the code style specific default value is set.
26→     */
27→    public val defaultValue: T,

32→     */
33→    public val ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
34→
35→    /**
36→     * Default value for property if it does not exist in loaded properties and codestyle 'intellij_idea'. When not
37→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
38→     */
39→    public val intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue: T = defaultValue,
40→

43→     * set, it is defaulted to [defaultValue].
44→     */

46→

48→     * If set, it maps the actual value set for the property, to another valid value for that property. See example

51→     * propertyMapper = { property, isAndroidCodeStyle ->
52→     *     when {
53→     *         property == null ->
54→     *             // property is not defined in ".editorconfig" file

56→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with special value "unset"
57→     *         property.sourceValue == "some-string-value" ->
58→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with a value that needs to be remapped to another
59→     *             // valid value. For example the "max_line_length" property accepts value "off" but is remapped to
60→     *             // "-1" in ktlint.
61→     *        else ->
62→     *             property.getValueAs() // or null
63→     *     }
64→     * }
65→     * ```
66→     * In case the lambda returns a null value then, the code style specific default value or the generic [defaultValue]
67→     * is used.

69→    public val propertyMapper: ((Property?, CodeStyleValue) -> T?)? = null,

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  5→import mu.KotlinLogging
  6→import org.ec4j.core.model.Property
  7→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
  8→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValue
  9→
 10→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * Loaded [Property]s from `.editorconfig` files.
 14→ */
 15→public data class EditorConfig(
 16→    private val properties: Map<String, Property> = emptyMap(),
 17→) {
 18→    public constructor(vararg properties: Property) : this(
 19→        properties.associateBy { property -> property.name },
 20→    )
 21→
 22→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue
 23→        get() =
 24→            with(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY) {
 25→                // Prevent printing a lot of warnings when the code_style_value is not defined explicitly by not using the function to get
 26→                // the default value for the property
 27→                type
 28→                    .getPropertyValue(type.name)
 29→                    .parsed
 30→                    ?: defaultValue
 31→            }
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Gets the value of [editorConfigProperty] from [EditorConfig] provided that the name of the property is identical to the name of the
 35→     * type of the property. If the value is not found, the default value for the matching code style is returned.
 36→     */
 37→    public operator fun <T> get(editorConfigProperty: EditorConfigProperty<T>): T {
 38→        when {
 39→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationError != null ->
 40→                throw DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
 41→                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is disallowed: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationError}",
 42→                )
 43→
 44→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning != null ->
 45→                LOGGER.warn { "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is deprecated: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning}" }
 46→        }
 47→        val property = properties.getOrElse(editorConfigProperty.name) {
 48→            throw IllegalStateException(
 49→                "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' can not be retrieved from this EditorConfig. Note that the EditorConfig which " +
 50→                    "is provided to class '${Rule::class.qualifiedName}' only contains the properties which are defined in the property " +
 51→                    "'${Rule::usesEditorConfigProperties.name}'.",
 52→            )
 53→        }
 54→
 55→        editorConfigProperty
 56→            .propertyMapper
 57→            ?.invoke(property, codeStyle)
 58→            ?.let { newValue ->
 59→                // If the property value is remapped to a non-null value then return it immediately.
 60→                val originalValue = property.sourceValue
 61→                if (newValue.toString() != originalValue) {
 62→                    LOGGER.trace {
 63→                        "Value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is remapped " +
 64→                            "from '$originalValue' to '$newValue'"
 65→                    }
 66→                }
 67→                return newValue
 68→            }
 69→
 70→        return if (property.isUnset) {
 71→            editorConfigProperty
 72→                .getDefaultValue()
 73→                .also {
 74→                    LOGGER.trace {
 75→                        "No value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' was found. Value has been defaulted to " +
 76→                            "'$it'. Setting the value explicitly in '.editorconfig' removes this message from the log."
 77→                    }
 78→                }
 79→        } else {
 80→            // In case the EditorConfig is loaded incorrectly it can happen that the type field of the loaded property is null. Getting the
 81→            // value via "property.getValueAs()" can lead to a class cast exception as the actual type of the value returned is transformed
 82→            // to a String instead of type T. Getting the value via the editorConfigProperty type is safe as it can not be null.
 83→            editorConfigProperty.getParsedValueOrDefault() ?: editorConfigProperty.getDefaultValue()
 84→        }
 85→    }
 86→
 87→    /**
 88→     * Gets the value of the property with [propertyType] and name [propertyName] from [EditorConfig]. Returns null if the type is not
 89→     * found. Also, returns null when no property with the name is found.
 90→     */
 91→    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(
 92→        propertyType: PropertyType<T>,
 93→        propertyName: String,
 94→    ): T? = propertyType.getPropertyValue(propertyName).parsed
 95→
 96→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 97→        when (codeStyle) {
 98→            CodeStyleValue.android -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
 99→            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
100→            CodeStyleValue.official -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101→            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
102→            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
103→        }
104→
105→    /**
106→     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
107→     */
108→    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)
109→
110→    /**
111→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
112→     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
113→     */
114→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
115→        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
116→
117→    /**
118→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
119→     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
120→     */
121→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
122→        propertyName: String,
123→        defaultValue: T,
124→    ): T =
125→        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
126→            .let { propertyValue ->
127→                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
128→                    propertyValue.parsed
129→                } else {
130→                    LOGGER.warn {
131→                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
132→                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
133→                    }
134→                    defaultValue
135→                }
136→            }
137→
138→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getPropertyValue(propertyName: String): PropertyValue<T> =
139→        parse(
140→            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
141→        )
142→
143→    /**
144→     * Maps all properties with given [mapper] to a collection.
145→     */
146→    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =
147→        properties
148→            .values
149→            .map { mapper(it) }
150→
151→    /**
152→     * Adds the given [EditorConfigProperty]'s with its default value for the active code style to the [EditorConfig] if the property does
153→     * not yet exist.
154→     */
155→    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
156→        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
157→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
158→        return editorConfigProperties
159→            .defaultProperties()
160→            .plus(
161→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
162→                properties,
163→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
164→    }
165→
166→    /**
167→     * Creates an [EditorConfig] containing [editorConfigProperties] only. Retains the value of the property when defined in the original
168→     * [EditorConfig]. If the property does not exist in the original [EditorConfig], it will be added and its value is set to the default
169→     * value of the active code style.
170→     */
171→    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
172→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
173→        return editorConfigProperties
174→            .defaultProperties()
175→            .plus(
176→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
177→                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),
178→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
179→    }
180→
181→    private fun requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>) {
182→        val editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities =
183→            editorConfigProperties
184→                .groupBy { it.name }
185→                .filterValues { groupedEditorConfigProperties ->
186→                    groupedEditorConfigProperties
187→                        .distinctBy { it.hashCode() }
188→                        .count() > 1
189→                }
190→        require(editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities.isEmpty()) {
191→            editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities
192→                .map { (name, editorConfigProperties) ->
193→                    editorConfigProperties
194→                        .joinToString(
195→                            separator = "\n",
196→                            prefix = "Found multiple editorconfig properties with name '$name' but having distinct identities:\n",
197→                        ) { "  - $it" }
198→                }.joinToString(separator = "\n")
199→        }
200→    }
201→
202→    private fun Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>.defaultProperties(): Map<String, Property> {
203→        return associate { editorConfigProperty ->
204→            editorConfigProperty
205→                .writeDefaultValue()
206→                .let { editorConfigProperty.name to editorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue(it) }
207→        }
208→    }
209→
210→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.writeDefaultValue() = propertyWriter(getDefaultValue())
211→
212→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.toPropertyWithValue(value: String): Property =
213→        Property
214→            .builder()
215→            .type(type)
216→            .name(name)
217→            .value(value)
218→            .build()
219→
220→    private fun Map<String, Property>.filterBy(editorConfigProperties: List<String>): Map<String, Property> =
221→        filterKeys { it in editorConfigProperties }
222→}
223→
224→private class DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(message: String) : RuntimeException(message)

140→
141→Starting with KtLint `0.48` entire rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set). 
142→
143→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
144→```editorconfig
145→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
146→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
147→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
148→```
149→
150→!!! note
151→    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All rules from the `experimental` rule set are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
152→
153→An individual property can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
154→```editorconfig
155→ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
156→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
157→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
158→```
159→
160→!!! note
161→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
162→
163→## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
164→
165→The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If ths property is not set explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.
166→

 4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule

 6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.CodeStyleValue

12→/**
13→ * Generates a Kotlin section content for the `.editorconfig` file. The set of properties is based on the properties used by the given set
14→ * of [Rule]s. The default values for missing properties are based on the given [CodeStyleValue].
15→ */
16→internal class EditorConfigGenerator(
17→    private val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
18→    private val editorConfigLoaderEc4j: EditorConfigLoaderEc4j,
19→) {
20→    /**
21→     * Method loads merged `.editorconfig` content using [filePath], and then, by querying the set of [Rule]s generates Kotlin section
22→     * (default is `[*.{kt,kts}]`) content including expected default values.
23→     *
24→     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
25→     * ```properties
26→     * final-newline = true
27→     * indent-size = 4
28→     * ```
29→     */
30→    fun generateEditorconfig(
31→        rules: Set<Rule>,
32→        codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
33→        filePath: Path,
34→    ): String =
35→        editorConfig(rules, codeStyle, filePath)
36→            .map { "${it.name} = ${it.sourceValue}" }
37→            .distinct()
38→            .sorted()
39→            .joinToString(separator = System.lineSeparator())
40→
41→    private fun editorConfig(
42→        rules: Set<Rule>,
43→        codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
44→        filePath: Path,
45→    ): EditorConfig {
46→        val usedEditorConfigProperties =
47→            rules
48→                .map { it.usesEditorConfigProperties }
49→                .flatten()
50→                .toSet()
51→        return loadEditorConfig(codeStyle, filePath)
52→            .filterBy(usedEditorConfigProperties.plus(DEFAULT_EDITOR_CONFIG_PROPERTIES))
53→    }
54→
55→    private fun loadEditorConfig(
56→        codeStyle: CodeStyleValue,
57→        filePath: Path,
58→    ) = EditorConfigLoader(
59→        fileSystem = fileSystem,
60→        editorConfigLoaderEc4j = editorConfigLoaderEc4j,
61→        editorConfigOverride = EditorConfigOverride.from(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY to codeStyle.name),
62→    ).load(filePath = filePath)
63→}

**Analyzing empty list handling for ktlint property**

I’m uncovering that generating an empty list for the ktlint wildcard imports property yields an unwanted empty assignment line, while ktlint docs say setting it to "nothing" disallows wildcard imports; however, parsing "nothing" treats it as a package pattern rather than empty, so this requires handling the special "nothing" value differently to correctly reflect disallowing imports.**Examining empty string parsing for wildcard imports**

I found that parsing an empty string as allowed wildcard imports produces a list with an empty-string entry, which may not properly reflect "no allowed wildcards," since the pattern entry with an empty package name still exists; this suggests the parser needs adjustment or special handling to treat empty strings as truly empty lists to align with disabling wildcard imports.

  4→
  5→/**
  6→ * Represents an entry in the imports layout pattern. Contains matching logic for imports.
  7→ *
  8→ * Adopted from https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinPackageEntry.kt#L10
  9→ */
 10→public class PatternEntry(
 11→    packageName: String,
 12→    public val withSubpackages: Boolean,
 13→    public val hasAlias: Boolean,
 14→) {
 15→    private val packageName = packageName.removeSuffix(".*")
 16→
 17→    private fun matchesPackageName(otherPackageName: String): Boolean {
 18→        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY || this == ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 19→        if (this == BLANK_LINE_ENTRY) return false
 20→
 21→        if (otherPackageName.startsWith(packageName)) {
 22→            if (otherPackageName.length == packageName.length) {
 23→                return true
 24→            }
 25→            if (withSubpackages && otherPackageName[packageName.length] == '.') {
 26→                return true
 27→            }
 28→        }
 29→        return false
 30→    }
 31→
 32→    internal fun matches(import: ImportPath): Boolean {
 33→        return matchesPackageName(import.pathStr.removeSuffix(".*"))
 34→    }
 35→
 36→    internal fun isBetterMatchForPackageThan(
 37→        entry: PatternEntry?,
 38→        import: ImportPath,
 39→    ): Boolean {
 40→        if (hasAlias != import.hasAlias() || !matchesPackageName(import.pathStr)) return false
 41→        if (entry == null) return true
 42→
 43→        if (entry.hasAlias != hasAlias) return false
 44→        // Any matched package is better than ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY
 45→        if (this == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return false
 46→        if (entry == ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY) return true
 47→
 48→        if (entry.withSubpackages != withSubpackages) return !withSubpackages
 49→
 50→        return entry.packageName.count { it == '.' } < packageName.count { it == '.' }
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    override fun toString(): String =
 54→        when (this) {
 55→            ALL_OTHER_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> WILDCARD_CHAR
 56→            ALL_OTHER_ALIAS_IMPORTS_ENTRY -> ALIAS_CHAR
 57→            else -> "$packageName.$WILDCARD_CHAR" + if (withSubpackages) WILDCARD_CHAR else ""
 58→        }
 59→
 60→    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean {
 61→        if (this === other) return true

content.thinking:
**Identifying risk of empty string wildcard**

I confirmed that an empty string as a package name causes matchesPackageName to return true for any package, enabling unintended wildcard matching. This means we must avoid empty strings in parsed properties to prevent mistakenly allowing all wildcards.**Planning to fix empty string parsing bug**

I found that parsing empty strings currently produces a wildcard match-all entry due to a trimming and filtering bug in parseAllowedWildcardImports. Updating it to trim and filter out blank entries will ensure empty or whitespace-only values parse to an empty list, preventing unintended wildcard matches.

  9→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 10→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 11→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.psi.KtImportDirective
 12→
 13→public class NoWildcardImportsRule :
 14→    StandardRule(
 15→        id = "no-wildcard-imports",
 16→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 17→            IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND,
 18→        ),
 19→    ) {
 20→    private lateinit var allowedWildcardImports: List<PatternEntry>
 21→
 22→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 23→        allowedWildcardImports = editorConfig[IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND]
 24→    }
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 27→        node: ASTNode,
 28→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 29→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 30→    ) {
 31→        if (node.elementType == IMPORT_DIRECTIVE) {
 32→            val importDirective = node.psi as KtImportDirective
 33→            val path = importDirective.importPath ?: return
 34→            if (!path.isAllUnder) return
 35→            if (allowedWildcardImports.none { it.matches(path) }) {
 36→                emit(node.startOffset, "Wildcard import", false)
 37→            }
 38→        }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    public companion object {
 42→        private const val WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES = "*"
 43→        private const val WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES = "**"
 44→
 45→        private fun parseAllowedWildcardImports(allowedWildcardImports: String): List<PatternEntry> {
 46→            val importsList = allowedWildcardImports.split(",").onEach { it.trim() }
 47→
 48→            return importsList.map { import ->
 49→                if (import.endsWith(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES)) { // java.**
 50→                    PatternEntry(
 51→                        packageName = import.removeSuffix(WILDCARD_WITH_SUBPACKAGES).plus(WILDCARD_WITHOUT_SUBPACKAGES),
 52→                        withSubpackages = true,
 53→                        hasAlias = false,
 54→                    )
 55→                } else {
 56→                    PatternEntry(
 57→                        packageName = import,
 58→                        withSubpackages = false,
 59→                        hasAlias = false,
 60→                    )
 61→                }
 62→            }
 63→        }
 64→
 65→        private val PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER: (String, String?) -> PropertyType.PropertyValue<List<PatternEntry>> =
 66→            { _, value ->
 67→                when {
 68→                    else -> try {
 69→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.valid(
 70→                            value,
 71→                            value?.let(Companion::parseAllowedWildcardImports) ?: emptyList(),
 72→                        )
 73→                    } catch (e: IllegalArgumentException) {
 74→                        PropertyType.PropertyValue.invalid(
 75→                            value,
 76→                            "Unexpected imports layout: $value",
 77→                        )
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→            }
 81→
 82→        public val IJ_KOTLIN_PACKAGES_TO_USE_IMPORT_ON_DEMAND: EditorConfigProperty<List<PatternEntry>> =
 83→            EditorConfigProperty(
 84→                type = PropertyType(
 85→                    "ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand",
 86→                    "Defines allowed wildcard imports",
 87→                    PACKAGES_TO_USE_ON_DEMAND_IMPORT_PROPERTY_PARSER,
 88→                ),
 89→                /**
 90→                 * Default IntelliJ IDEA style: Use wildcard imports for packages in "java.util", "kotlin.android.synthetic" and
 91→                 * it's subpackages.
 92→                 *
 93→                 * https://github.com/JetBrains/kotlin/blob/ffdab473e28d0d872136b910eb2e0f4beea2e19c/idea/formatter/src/org/jetbrains/kotlin/idea/core/formatter/KotlinCodeStyleSettings.java#L81-L82
 94→                 */
 95→                defaultValue = parseAllowedWildcardImports("java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**"),
 96→                propertyWriter = { it.joinToString(separator = ",") },
 97→            )
 98→    }
 99→}
100→
101→public val NO_WILDCARD_IMPORTS_RULE_ID: RuleId = NoWildcardImportsRule().ruleId

CHANGELOG.md:41:The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
--
CHANGELOG.md:43:The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
--
CHANGELOG.md:227:* Add new experimental rule `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows a class to start with a blank line. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
CHANGELOG.md:228:* Add new experimental rule `if-else-bracing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rules enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
CHANGELOG.md:229:* Add new experimental rule `no-consecutive-comments` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
CHANGELOG.md:230:* Add new experimental rule `try-catch-finally-spacing` for `ktlint_official` code style. This rule enforces consistent spacing in try-catch, try-finally and try-catch-finally statement. This rule can also be run for other code styles but then its needs to be explicitly enabled.
--

 26→internal class RuleExecutionContext private constructor(
 27→    val code: Code,
 28→    val rootNode: FileASTNode,
 29→    val ruleRunners: Set<RuleRunner>,
 30→    val editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 31→    val positionInTextLocator: (offset: Int) -> LineAndColumn,
 32→) {
 33→    private lateinit var suppressionLocator: SuppressionLocator
 34→
 35→    init {
 36→        rebuildSuppressionLocator()
 37→    }
 38→
 39→    fun rebuildSuppressionLocator() {
 40→        suppressionLocator = SuppressionLocatorBuilder.buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(rootNode)
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    fun executeRule(
 44→        rule: Rule,
 45→        fqRuleId: String,
 46→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 47→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 48→    ) {
 49→        try {
 50→            rule.startTraversalOfAST()
 51→            rule.beforeFirstNode(
 52→                // The rule get access to an EditConfig which is filtered by the properties which are actually registered as being used by
 53→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
 54→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
 55→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties),
 56→            )
 57→            this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(rootNode, rule, fqRuleId, autoCorrect, emit)
 58→            rule.afterLastNode()
 59→        } catch (e: RuleExecutionException) {
 60→            throw KtLintRuleException(
 61→                e.line,
 62→                e.col,
 63→                e.rule.ruleId.value,
 64→                """
 65→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileName}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
 66→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
 67→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
 68→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
 69→                """.trimIndent(),
 70→                e.cause,
 71→            )
 72→        }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    private fun executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 76→        node: ASTNode,
 77→        rule: Rule,
 78→        fqRuleId: String,
 79→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        /**
 83→         * The [suppressionLocator] can be changed during each visit of node when running [KtLintRuleEngine.format]. So a new handler is to
 84→         * be built before visiting the nodes.
 85→         */
 86→        val suppressHandler = SuppressHandler(suppressionLocator, autoCorrect, emit)
 87→        if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 88→            try {
 89→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 90→                    rule.beforeVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
 91→                }
 92→                if (rule.shouldContinueTraversalOfAST()) {
 93→                    node
 94→                        .getChildren(null)
 95→                        .forEach { childNode ->
 96→                            suppressHandler.handle(childNode, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
 97→                                this.executeRuleOnNodeRecursively(
 98→                                    childNode,
 99→                                    rule,
100→                                    fqRuleId,
101→                                    autoCorrect,
102→                                    emit,
103→                                )
104→                            }
105→                        }
106→                }
107→                suppressHandler.handle(node, rule.ruleId) { autoCorrect, emit ->
108→                    rule.afterVisitChildNodes(node, autoCorrect, emit)
109→                }
110→            } catch (e: Exception) {
111→                if (autoCorrect) {
112→                    // line/col cannot be reliably mapped as exception might originate from a node not present in the
113→                    // original AST
114→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
115→                        rule,
116→                        0,
117→                        0,
118→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
119→                        e.cause ?: e,
120→                    )
121→                } else {
122→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(node.startOffset)
123→                    throw RuleExecutionException(
124→                        rule,
125→                        line,
126→                        col,
127→                        // Prevent extreme long stack trace caused by recursive call and only pass root cause
128→                        e.cause ?: e,
129→                    )
130→                }
131→            }
132→        }
133→    }
134→
135→    companion object {
136→        internal fun createRuleExecutionContext(
137→            ktLintRuleEngine: KtLintRuleEngine,
138→            code: Code,
139→        ): RuleExecutionContext {
140→            val psiFileFactory = KOTLIN_PSI_FILE_FACTORY_PROVIDER.getKotlinPsiFileFactory(ktLintRuleEngine.isInvokedFromCli)
141→            val normalizedText = normalizeText(code.content)
142→            val positionInTextLocator = buildPositionInTextLocator(normalizedText)
143→
144→            val psiFileName =
145→                code.fileName
146→                    ?: if (code.script) {
147→                        "File.kts"
148→                    } else {
149→                        "File.kt"
150→                    }
151→            val psiFile = psiFileFactory.createFileFromText(
152→                psiFileName,
153→                KotlinLanguage.INSTANCE,
154→                normalizedText,
155→            ) as KtFile
156→
157→            psiFile
158→                .findErrorElement()
159→                ?.let { errorElement ->
160→                    val (line, col) = positionInTextLocator(errorElement.textOffset)
161→                    throw KtLintParseException(line, col, errorElement.errorDescription)
162→                }
163→
164→            val rootNode = psiFile.node
165→
166→            val editorConfig =
167→                ktLintRuleEngine
168→                    .editorConfigLoader
169→                    .load(code.filePath)
170→                    .also {
171→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
172→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("disabled_rules", "0.49")
173→                        // TODO: Remove warning below in KtLint 0.52 or later as some users skips multiple versions
174→                        it.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete("ktlint_disabled_rules", "0.49")
175→                    }
176→
177→            val ruleRunners =
178→                ktLintRuleEngine.ruleRunners(
179→                    RuleExecutionRuleFilter(editorConfig),
180→                    RunAfterRuleFilter(),
181→                )
182→
183→            return RuleExecutionContext(
184→                code,
185→                rootNode,
186→                ruleRunners,
187→                editorConfig,
188→                positionInTextLocator,
189→            )
190→        }
191→
192→        private fun normalizeText(text: String): String {
193→            return text
194→                .replace("\r\n", "\n")
195→                .replace("\r", "\n")
196→                .replaceFirst(UTF8_BOM, "")
197→        }
198→
199→        private fun PsiElement.findErrorElement(): PsiErrorElement? {
200→            if (this is PsiErrorElement) {
201→                return this
202→            }
203→            this.children.forEach { child ->
204→                val errorElement = child.findErrorElement()
205→                if (errorElement != null) {
206→                    return errorElement
207→                }
208→            }
209→            return null
210→        }
211→    }
212→}

214→private fun EditorConfig.warnIfPropertyIsObsolete(
215→    propertyName: String,
216→    ktlintVersion: String,
217→) {
218→    if (this.contains(propertyName)) {
219→        LOGGER.warn {
220→            "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' is obsolete and is not used by KtLint starting from version $ktlintVersion. Remove " +
221→                "the property from all '.editorconfig' files."
222→        }
223→    }
224→}

 30→
 31→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 32→
 33→/**
 34→ * Loader for `.editorconfig` properties for files on [fileSystem].
 35→ */
 36→internal class EditorConfigLoader(
 37→    private val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 38→    private val editorConfigLoaderEc4j: EditorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 39→    private val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 40→    private val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 41→) {
 42→    /**
 43→     * Loads properties used by [Rule]s from the `.editorconfig` file on given [filePath]. When [filePath] is null, the
 44→     * properties for the ".kt" pattern in the current directory are loaded. The '.editorconfig' files on the [filePath]
 45→     * are read starting from the [filePath] upwards until an '.editorconfig' file is found in which the property "root"
 46→     * is found with value "true" or until the root of the filesystem is reached.
 47→     *
 48→     * Properties specified in [editorConfigDefaults] will be used in case the property was not found in any
 49→     * '.editorconfig' on [filePath]. If the property is not specified in [editorConfigDefaults] then the default value
 50→     * as specified in the property definition [EditorConfigProperty] is used.
 51→     *
 52→     * Properties specified in [editorConfigOverride] take precedence above any other '.editorconfig' file on [filePath]
 53→     * or default value.
 54→     */
 55→    internal fun load(filePath: Path?): EditorConfig {
 56→        val editorConfigPath = filePath ?: defaultFilePath()
 57→        val editorConfigProperties: MutableMap<String, Property> =
 58→            createResourcePropertiesService(editorConfigLoaderEc4j.editorConfigLoader, editorConfigDefaults)
 59→                .queryProperties(editorConfigPath.resource())
 60→                .properties
 61→        return editorConfigProperties
 62→            .also { properties ->
 63→                if (properties[TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME]?.sourceValue == properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY.name]?.sourceValue &&
 64→                    editorConfigOverride.properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY] != null
 65→                ) {
 66→                    // The tab_width property can not be overridden via the editorConfigOverride. So if it has been
 67→                    // set to the same value as the indent_size property then keep its value in sync with that
 68→                    // property.
 69→                    properties[TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME] =
 70→                        Property
 71→                            .builder()
 72→                            .name(TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME)
 73→                            .type(PropertyType.tab_width)
 74→                            .value(editorConfigOverride.properties[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY]?.source)
 75→                            .build()
 76→                }
 77→                editorConfigOverride
 78→                    .properties
 79→                    .forEach {
 80→                        properties[it.key.name] = it.key.toPropertyWithValue(it.value)
 81→                    }
 82→            }.also { properties ->
 83→                LOGGER.debug { properties.prettyPrint(filePath) }
 84→            }.let { properties ->
 85→                // Only add properties which are not related to rules but which are needed by the KtLint Rule Engine
 86→                EditorConfig(properties)
 87→                    .addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(
 88→                        /**
 89→                         * Used in [EditorConfig] to determine the default value for a property when it is not specified in the
 90→                         * [EditorConfig].
 91→                         */
 92→                        CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 93→                        /**
 94→                         * Used by [KtLintRuleEngine] to use correct line separator when writing the formatted output.
 95→                         */
 96→                        END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY,
 97→                        /**
 98→                         * Used by [VisitorProvider] to determine whether experimental rules have to be executed.
 99→                         */
100→                        EXPERIMENTAL_RULES_EXECUTION_PROPERTY,
101→                    )
102→            }
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun MutableMap<String, Property>.prettyPrint(normalizedFilePath: Path?) =
106→        map { entry -> "${entry.key}: ${entry.value.sourceValue}" }
107→            .joinToString(
108→                prefix = "Effective editorconfig properties${
109→                    if (normalizedFilePath == null) {
110→                        ""
111→                    } else {
112→                        " for file '$normalizedFilePath'"
113→                    }
114→                }:\n\t",
115→                separator = "\n\t",
116→            )
117→
118→    private fun Path?.resource() = Resource.Resources.ofPath(this, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
119→
120→    private fun defaultFilePath() =
121→        fileSystem
122→            .getPath(".")
123→            .toAbsolutePath()
124→            .resolve(SUPPORTED_FILES.first())
125→
126→    private fun createResourcePropertiesService(
127→        editorConfigLoader: EditorConfigLoader,
128→        editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults,
129→    ) = ResourcePropertiesService
130→        .builder()
131→        .keepUnset(true)
132→        .cache(THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE)
133→        .loader(editorConfigLoader)
134→        .applyIf(editorConfigDefaults != EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS) {
135→            defaultEditorConfigs(editorConfigDefaults.value)
136→        }.build()
137→
138→    companion object {
139→        /**
140→         * List of file extensions, editorconfig lookup will be performed.
141→         */
142→        internal val SUPPORTED_FILES = arrayOf(
143→            ".kt",
144→            ".kts",
145→        )
146→
147→        private const val TAB_WIDTH_PROPERTY_NAME = "tab_width"
148→    }

151→/**
152→ * Wrapper around the native EditorConfigLoader of the ec4j library. Whenever Ktlints loads ".editorconfig" files via the ec4j library, it
153→ * needs to be aware of all native (ec4j) but also the custom property types defined in KtLint and the custom rules sets provided to KtLint.
154→ *
155→ * The ec4j library uses the convention that a property type has a name that is identical to the name of that property. If no
156→ * property type is registered for a property the property will be returned with a null type. When retrieving the property value it will be
157→ * returned as type "String" which results in class cast exceptions in KtLint's EditorConfig whenever the expected type of the variable is
158→ * not of type String.
159→ */
160→internal class EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(
161→    private val propertyTypes: Set<PropertyType<*>>,
162→) {
163→    val editorConfigLoader: EditorConfigLoader
164→        get() = EditorConfigLoader.of(Version.CURRENT, propertyTypeRegistry(propertyTypes))
165→
166→    private fun propertyTypeRegistry(propertyTypes: Set<PropertyType<*>>) =
167→        PropertyTypeRegistry
168→            .builder()
169→            .defaults()
170→            .apply {
171→                propertyTypes.forEach { type(it) }
172→            }.build()
173→}

 9→/**
10→ * Wrapper around the ec4j [EditorConfig]. Only to be used to specify the default value of ec4j properties. Those default values will only
11→ * be used whenever a property is retrieved from the ec4j [EditorConfig] and the property has not been defined in the ".editorconfig" file.
12→ */
13→public data class EditorConfigDefaults(
14→    /**
15→     * The ec4j [EditorConfig] containing the default value of the ec4j properties. Those default values will only be used whenever a
16→     * property is retrieved from the ec4j [EditorConfig] and the property has not been defined in the ".editorconfig" file.
17→     */
18→    public val value: EditorConfig,
19→) {
20→    public companion object {
21→        /**
22→         * Loads properties from [path]. [path] may either locate a file (also allows specifying a file with a name other
23→         * than ".editorconfig") or a directory in which a file with name ".editorconfig" is expected to exist. Properties
24→         * from all globs are returned.
25→         *
26→         * If [path] is not valid then the [EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS] is returned.
27→         *
28→         * The property "root" which denotes whether the parent directory is to be checked for the existence of a fallback
29→         * ".editorconfig" is ignored entirely.
30→         */
31→        @Deprecated(
32→            message = "Marked for removal in Ktlint 0.50. This method causes class cast exceptions in case the '.editorconfig' file " +
33→                "contains properties having an EditorConfigProperty with a custom type (e.g. a type not defined in the ec4j library)",
34→            replaceWith = ReplaceWith("load(path, propertyTypes)"),
35→        )
36→        public fun load(path: Path?): EditorConfigDefaults =
37→            if (path == null) {
38→                EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS
39→            } else {
40→                EditorConfigDefaultsLoader(
41→                    EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(emptySet()),
42→                ).load(path)
43→            }
44→
45→        /**
46→         * Loads properties from [path]. [path] may either locate a file (also allows specifying a file with a name other than
47→         * ".editorconfig") or a directory in which a file with name ".editorconfig" is expected to exist. Properties from all globs are
48→         * returned. If [path] is not valid then the [EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS] is returned.
49→         *
50→         * Properties having a custom [PropertyType] (e.g. a type not defined in the ec4j library) can not be parsed to the correct type
51→         * when the property type is missing and will by default be converted to type [String]. [PropertyType]'s can be easily extracted
52→         * with extension function `Collection<RuleProvider>.propertyTypes()`.
53→         *
54→         * The property "root" which denotes whether the parent directory is to be checked for the existence of a fallback
55→         * ".editorconfig" is ignored entirely.
56→         */
57→        public fun load(
58→            path: Path?,
59→            propertyTypes: Set<PropertyType<*>>,
60→        ): EditorConfigDefaults =
61→            if (path == null) {
62→                EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS
63→            } else {
64→                EditorConfigDefaultsLoader(
65→                    EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(propertyTypes),
66→                ).load(path)
67→            }
68→
69→        /**
70→         * Empty representation of [EditorConfigDefaults].
71→         */
72→        public val EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS: EditorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults(EditorConfig.builder().build())
73→    }

 34→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 35→
 36→public class KtLintRuleEngine(
 37→    /**
 38→     * The set of [RuleProvider]s to be invoked by the [KtLintRuleEngine]. A [RuleProvider] is able to create a new instance of a [Rule] so
 39→     * that it can keep internal state and be called thread-safe manner
 40→     */
 41→    public val ruleProviders: Set<RuleProvider> = emptySet(),
 42→
 43→    /**
 44→     * The default values for `.editorconfig` properties which are not set explicitly in any '.editorconfig' file located on the path of the
 45→     * file which is processed with the [KtLintRuleEngine]. If a property is set in [editorConfigDefaults] this takes precedence above the
 46→     * default values defined in the KtLint project.
 47→     */
 48→    public val editorConfigDefaults: EditorConfigDefaults = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_DEFAULTS,
 49→
 50→    /**
 51→     * Override values for `.editorconfig` properties. If a property is set in [editorConfigOverride] it takes precedence above the same
 52→     * property being set in any other way.
 53→     */
 54→    public val editorConfigOverride: EditorConfigOverride = EMPTY_EDITOR_CONFIG_OVERRIDE,
 55→
 56→    /**
 57→     * **For internal use only**: indicates that linting was invoked from KtLint CLI tool. It enables some internals workarounds for Kotlin
 58→     * Compiler initialization. This property is likely to be removed in any of next versions without further notice.
 59→     */
 60→    public val isInvokedFromCli: Boolean = false,
 61→
 62→    /**
 63→     * The [FileSystem] to be used. This property is primarily intended to be used in unit tests. By specifying an alternative [FileSystem]
 64→     * the unit test gains control on whether the [EditorConfigLoader] should or should not read specific ".editorconfig" files. For
 65→     * example, it is considered unwanted that a unit test is influenced by the ".editorconfig" file of the project in which the unit test
 66→     * is included.
 67→     */
 68→    public val fileSystem: FileSystem = FileSystems.getDefault(),
 69→) {
 70→    init {
 71→        require(ruleProviders.any()) {
 72→            "A non-empty set of 'ruleProviders' need to be provided"
 73→        }
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    internal val editorConfigLoaderEc4j = EditorConfigLoaderEc4j(ruleProviders.propertyTypes())
 77→
 78→    internal val editorConfigLoader = EditorConfigLoader(
 79→        fileSystem,
 80→        editorConfigLoaderEc4j,
 81→        editorConfigDefaults,
 82→        editorConfigOverride,
 83→    )
 84→
 85→    /**
 86→     * Check the [code] for lint errors. If [code] is path as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on the path to file are taken
 87→     * into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
 88→     *
 89→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
 90→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
 91→     */
 92→    public fun lint(
 93→        code: Code,
 94→        callback: (LintError) -> Unit = { },
 95→    ) {
 96→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with linting file '${code.fileName}'" }
 97→
 98→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
 99→        val errors = mutableListOf<LintError>()
100→
101→        VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleRunners)
102→            .visitor()
103→            .invoke { rule, fqRuleId ->
104→                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, fqRuleId, false) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
105→                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
106→                    errors.add(LintError(line, col, fqRuleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected))
107→                }
108→            }
109→
110→        errors
111→            .sortedWith { l, r -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
112→            .forEach { e -> callback(e) }
113→
114→        LOGGER.debug { "Finished with linting file '${code.fileName}'" }
115→    }
116→
117→    /**
118→     * Fix style violations in [code] for lint errors when possible. If [code] is passed as file reference then the '.editorconfig' files on
119→     * the path are taken into account. For each lint violation found, the [callback] is invoked.
120→     *
121→     * @throws KtLintParseException if text is not a valid Kotlin code
122→     * @throws KtLintRuleException in case of internal failure caused by a bug in rule implementation
123→     */
124→    public fun format(
125→        code: Code,
126→        callback: (LintError, Boolean) -> Unit = { _, _ -> },
127→    ): String {
128→        LOGGER.debug { "Starting with formatting file '${code.fileName}'" }
129→
130→        val hasUTF8BOM = code.content.startsWith(UTF8_BOM)
131→        val ruleExecutionContext = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code)
132→
133→        var tripped = false
134→        var mutated = false
135→        val errors = mutableSetOf<Pair<LintError, Boolean>>()
136→        val visitorProvider = VisitorProvider(ruleExecutionContext.ruleRunners)
137→        visitorProvider
138→            .visitor()
139→            .invoke { rule, fqRuleId ->
140→                ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(rule, fqRuleId, true) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
141→                    tripped = true
142→                    if (canBeAutoCorrected) {
143→                        mutated = true
144→                        /**
145→                         * Rebuild the suppression locator after each change in the AST as the offsets of the
146→                         * suppression hints might have changed.
147→                         */
148→                        ruleExecutionContext.rebuildSuppressionLocator()
149→                    }
150→                    val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
151→                    errors.add(
152→                        Pair(
153→                            LintError(line, col, fqRuleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
154→                            // It is assumed that a rule that emits that an error can be autocorrected, also
155→                            // does correct the error.
156→                            canBeAutoCorrected,
157→                        ),
158→                    )
159→                }
160→            }
161→        if (tripped) {
162→            visitorProvider
163→                .visitor()
164→                .invoke { rule, fqRuleId ->
165→                    ruleExecutionContext.executeRule(
166→                        rule,
167→                        fqRuleId,
168→                        false,
169→                    ) { offset, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected ->
170→                        val (line, col) = ruleExecutionContext.positionInTextLocator(offset)
171→                        errors.add(
172→                            Pair(
173→                                LintError(line, col, fqRuleId, errorMessage, canBeAutoCorrected),
174→                                // It is assumed that a rule only corrects an error after it has emitted an
175→                                // error and indicating that it actually can be autocorrected.
176→                                false,
177→                            ),
178→                        )
179→                    }
180→                }
181→        }
182→
183→        errors
184→            .sortedWith { (l), (r) -> if (l.line != r.line) l.line - r.line else l.col - r.col }
185→            .forEach { (e, corrected) -> callback(e, corrected) }
186→
187→        if (!mutated) {
188→            return code.content
189→        }
190→
191→        val formattedCode = ruleExecutionContext
192→            .rootNode
193→            .text
194→            .replace("\n", ruleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(code.content))
195→        return if (hasUTF8BOM) {
196→            UTF8_BOM + formattedCode
197→        } else {
198→            formattedCode
199→        }.also {
200→            LOGGER.debug("Finished with formatting file '${code.fileName}'")
201→        }
202→    }
203→
204→    private fun RuleExecutionContext.determineLineSeparator(fileContent: String): String {
205→        val eol = editorConfig[END_OF_LINE_PROPERTY]
206→        return when {
207→            eol == crlf || eol != lf && fileContent.lastIndexOf('\r') != -1 -> "\r\n"
208→            else -> "\n"
209→        }
210→    }
211→
212→    /**
213→     * Generates Kotlin `.editorconfig` file section content based on a path to a file or directory. Given that path, all '.editorconfig'
214→     * files on that path are taken into account to determine the values of properties which are already used.
215→     *
216→     * @return Kotlin section editorconfig content. For example:
217→     * ```properties
218→     * final-newline=true
219→     * indent-size=4
220→     * ```
221→     */
222→    public fun generateKotlinEditorConfigSection(filePath: Path): String {
223→        val codeStyle =
224→            editorConfigOverride
225→                .properties[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
226→                ?.parsed
227→                ?.safeAs<CodeStyleValue>()
228→                ?: CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
229→        val rules =
230→            ruleProviders
231→                .map { RuleRunner(it) }
232→                .distinctBy { it.ruleId }
233→                .toSet()
234→                .map { it.getRule() }
235→                .toSet()
236→        return EditorConfigGenerator(fileSystem, editorConfigLoaderEc4j)
237→            .generateEditorconfig(
238→                rules,
239→                codeStyle,
240→                filePath,
241→            )
242→    }
243→
244→    /**
245→     * Reduce memory usage by cleaning internal caches.
246→     */
247→    public fun trimMemory() {
248→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.clear()
249→    }
250→
251→    /**
252→     * Get the list of files which will be accessed by KtLint when linting or formatting the given file or directory.
253→     * The API consumer can use this list to observe changes in '.editorconfig' files. Whenever such a change is
254→     * observed, the API consumer should call [reloadEditorConfigFile].
255→     * To avoid unnecessary access to the file system, it is best to call this method only once for the root of the
256→     * project which is to be [lint] or [format].
257→     */
258→    public fun editorConfigFilePaths(path: Path): List<Path> = EditorConfigFinder(editorConfigLoaderEc4j).findEditorConfigs(path)
259→
260→    /**
261→     * Reloads an '.editorconfig' file given that it is currently loaded into the KtLint cache. This method is intended
262→     * to be called by the API consumer when it is aware of changes in the '.editorconfig' file that should be taken
263→     * into account with next calls to [lint] and/or [format]. See [editorConfigFilePaths] to get the list of
264→     * '.editorconfig' files which need to be observed.
265→     */
266→    public fun reloadEditorConfigFile(path: Path) {
267→        THREAD_SAFE_EDITOR_CONFIG_CACHE.reloadIfExists(
268→            Resource.Resources.ofPath(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8),
269→        )
270→    }
271→
272→    public fun transformToAst(code: Code): FileASTNode = createRuleExecutionContext(this, code).rootNode
273→
274→    public companion object {
275→        internal const val UTF8_BOM = "\uFEFF"
276→
277→        public const val STDIN_FILE: String = "<stdin>"
278→    }
279→}

CHANGELOG.md:41:The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
